athlete_objective: FTP-priority macrocycle: become the best possible all-round cyclist with the highest sustainable FTP realistically possible, with 300 W by September 1, 2026 as an explicit stretch target. Accept controlled overload when recovery supports it.
planning_notes: This is an FTP-priority macrocycle, not a maintenance plan. Weekly target events guide Intervals fitness/form evolution; they are not fixed daily workouts.
Detailed workouts should be regenerated every 2-4 weeks from actual execution, wellness, weather, family calendar, and power evidence.

Normal build weeks should usually include two purposeful bike stimuli: one threshold/VO2/over-under stimulus and one tempo/sweet-spot durability stimulus, plus a long endurance ride and strength maintenance.

Through September 1, 2026, do not treat 260 W as a comfortable ceiling. Use progressive training blocks that make 300 W possible: frequent sustained aerobic/tempo volume, weekly threshold-adjacent work, overload weeks when wellness is stable, and validation only when it informs the next FTP step.
About 70-80% of riding time should remain easy across each block; the higher ambition comes mainly from more absorbable volume and repeatable sustained work, not random intensity.
Recovery weeks are mandatory. If RPE, Feel, sleep, HRV/resting HR, or Form show poor absorption, lower the week before protecting the headline load.
June 2026 was corrected more aggressively after Intervals projection showed Form stayed above -10 until month-end despite the FTP-priority objective.

targets.name: ATP W01 - Build continuation
targets.description: Annual macro target week 1/52. Phase: Build continuation. Focus: Tempo durability, long aerobic ride, endurance-skill ride, strength maintenance. This is a weekly target, not a fixed workout prescription. Generate exact workouts adaptively from recent execution and recovery. Objective guardrail: FTP-priority development through controlled overload, repeatable sustained power, aerobic durability, and recovery discipline; not chart-chasing. Default FTP-priority week: one threshold/VO2/over-under stimulus, one tempo or sweet-spot durability stimulus, one long endurance ride, and one strength-maintenance session when recovery supports it.

targets.name: ATP W02 - 300W progression build
targets.description: Annual macro target week 2/52. Phase: 300W progression build. Focus: Three completed bike rides only after Thursday, Friday, and Saturday were blocked; weekend hike plus cottage tree/trench work counts as fatigue context, not FTP-specific bike load. No catch-up debt. Generate exact workouts adaptively from recent execution and recovery. Objective guardrail: FTP-priority development toward the 300 W September 1 target through controlled overload, repeatable sustained power, aerobic durability, and recovery discipline; not chart-chasing.

targets.name: ATP W03 - 300W aerobic power build
targets.description: Annual macro target week 3/52. Phase: 300W aerobic power build. Focus: Start with a recovery-biased Monday check after cottage manual work, then preserve Tuesday volume, Wednesday sweet spot if legs/back are normal, and a Friday sustained-power anchor so the week does not depend on cottage bike access. This is a weekly target, not a fixed workout prescription. Generate exact workouts adaptively from recent execution and recovery. Objective guardrail: FTP-priority development toward the 300 W September 1 target through controlled overload, repeatable sustained power, aerobic durability, and recovery discipline; not chart-chasing. Adjusted 2026-06-15: load/hours moderated from the original 340 to ramp from the actual CTL ~32.5 (down from ~37) after a disrupted week + cottage manual labor; ramp gate wins over the table. FTP stays 260 (athlete-confirmed).

targets.name: ATP W04 - 300W overload preparation
targets.description: Annual macro target week 4/52. Phase: 300W overload preparation. Focus: Over-under progression plus a Friday overload anchor, enough load to enter controlled green if recovery is stable, with weekend cottage workouts optional only if access is confirmed. This is a weekly target, not a fixed workout prescription. Generate exact workouts adaptively from recent execution and recovery. Objective guardrail: FTP-priority development toward the 300 W September 1 target through controlled overload, repeatable sustained power, aerobic durability, and recovery discipline; not chart-chasing. Adjusted 2026-06-15: load/hours moderated from the original 395 to ramp from the actual CTL ~32.5 (down from ~37) after a disrupted week + cottage manual labor; ramp gate wins over the table. FTP stays 260 (athlete-confirmed).

targets.name: ATP W05 - Controlled overload
targets.description: Annual macro target week 5/52. Phase: Controlled overload. Focus: Highest load of the June wave; one threshold/VO2 stimulus, one sustained tempo/durability anchor, and long aerobic support if recovery markers stay stable. This is a weekly target, not a fixed workout prescription. Generate exact workouts adaptively from recent execution and recovery. Objective guardrail: FTP-priority development toward the 300 W September 1 target through controlled overload, repeatable sustained power, aerobic durability, and recovery discipline; not chart-chasing. Adjusted 2026-06-15: load/hours moderated from the original 430 to ramp from the actual CTL ~32.5 (down from ~37) after a disrupted week + cottage manual labor; ramp gate wins over the table. FTP stays 260 (athlete-confirmed).
